sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 84:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 84:1
items.html: It is the inversion of the anterior of the vessel of Hochma downward and the making of the posterior, which is illumination of VAK, into her anterior, like the anterior of the vessel of Bina. By so doing, Hochma was divided into two phases, meaning bestowal of GAR and bestowal of VAK. The vessel Hochma itself is considered bestowal of GAR, and the vessel of Bina, which received VAK of Hochma, is considered bestowal of VAK. (Inner Light, Item‎ 40)
